title: ageratina cuencana
intro: Ageratina cuencana, also known as Cuenca ageratina or Hierba del Venado, is a plant species native to South America, specifically found in Ecuador and Peru. It is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ageratina cuencana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
